Eureka

Eureka is a Python reference backend prototype for evidence-backed archive resolution, with a current usefulness wedge around old-platform-compatible software search and member-level discovery inside bundles.

Overview Status Snapshot Source Matrix Eval And Audit Status Demo Queries Limitations And Public-Alpha Boundaries Live-Alpha Preparation Roadmap Local Quickstart
Current maturity: Eureka is not production. This generated static output is public-facing documentation only; it does not deploy a backend or run live searches.

What It Is

A local-first reference backend that records sources, plans bounded archive/software queries, exposes result lanes, compatibility evidence, member records, and eval reports through CLI, HTTP API, and server-rendered web surfaces.

What It Is Not

It is not broad live archive search, not a native GUI app, not an app-store product, and not a production hosted service.

Current Demo Shape

The safe demo corpus is fixture-backed: synthetic fixtures, recorded GitHub release fixtures, Internet-Archive-shaped recorded fixtures, local bundle fixtures, and article/scan recorded fixtures.

Useful Starting Points

  • Try the local CLI/API/web workbench
  • Review the source matrix
  • Read the eval and audit status
  • Check public-alpha limitations
  • GitHub repository
Eureka is a Python reference backend prototype, not production. This site uses no scraping, performs no live source probes, and makes no external search calls. external baselines pending/manual; source placeholders remain placeholders.
